scSidekick - Survival Analysis: TCGA & Custom Cohorts

15_survival.Rmd

Overview

scSidekick provides a complete Kaplan-Meier survival analysis pipeline for bulk RNA-seq expression data. Four functions cover the full workflow:

Function What it does
LoadTCGA() Download and cache a TCGA or TARGET expression dataset
EnrichTCGAMeta() Add GDC clinical + molecular subtype columns to metadata
SurvMetaSummary() Explore available clinical columns before running survival analysis
SurvPlot() Kaplan-Meier plots for genes or gene-set signatures, with optional faceting and multivariate Cox

Two input modes:

  • TCGA mode - pass tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM" and data are downloaded, processed, and cached automatically. Subsequent calls load instantly from cache.
  • Custom matrix mode - pass your own expr_mat (genes x samples) and meta (samples x variables). Works for any bulk RNA-seq or microarray cohort.

Required packages 

# Core survival packages
install.packages(c("survival", "survminer"))

# For TCGA downloads (recommended backend - single consolidated download)
BiocManager::install("recount3")

# For richer clinical metadata and molecular subtypes (optional but recommended)
BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks")

Part 1: Loading TCGA Data

1.1 Download a TCGA project

LoadTCGA() fetches the full expression matrix and clinical metadata for any TCGA or TARGET project and caches the result locally. The default backend is recount3, which downloads a single consolidated file per project - much faster and more reliable than the per-patient GDC downloads.

library(scSidekick)

# Download TCGA-GBM (glioblastoma) - ~173 patients
# First call downloads; subsequent calls load from cache instantly
gbm <- LoadTCGA("TCGA-GBM")

# Access the expression matrix (genes x samples, log2 TPM+1)
dim(gbm$expr_mat)

# Access clinical metadata
head(gbm$meta[, c("OS", "status", "gender")])

The returned list contains: - $expr_mat - numeric matrix, genes x samples, log2(TPM + 1) - $meta - data frame of clinical metadata, one row per sample - $project, $data_type, $backend - provenance fields

1.2 Available TCGA projects

Pass any "TCGA-<CODE>" string to tcga_project. Common cancer types:

Code Cancer type n
"TCGA-GBM" Glioblastoma ~173
"TCGA-LGG" Lower grade glioma ~516
"TCGA-BRCA" Breast invasive carcinoma ~1,098
"TCGA-LUAD" Lung adenocarcinoma ~585
"TCGA-LUSC" Lung squamous cell ~504
"TCGA-SKCM" Skin cutaneous melanoma ~473
"TCGA-PRAD" Prostate adenocarcinoma ~551
"TCGA-KIRC" Kidney clear cell ~611
"TCGA-OV" Ovarian carcinoma ~608
"TCGA-LAML" Acute myeloid leukemia ~200

Pediatric TARGET projects: "TARGET-AML", "TARGET-NBL", "TARGET-OS", "TARGET-WT", "TARGET-RT", "TARGET-CCSK".

1.3 Forcing a fresh download 

# Re-download and update the cache
gbm <- LoadTCGA("TCGA-GBM", force_reload = TRUE)

1.4 Microarray data

For older TCGA accessions or external microarray cohorts, set data_type = "microarray". This automatically switches to the TCGAbiolinks backend (recount3 only carries RNA-seq).

gbm_array <- LoadTCGA("TCGA-GBM", data_type = "microarray")

Part 2: Enriching Metadata

2.1 Add GDC clinical and molecular subtype columns

The recount3 download includes basic clinical columns (OS, status, gender). EnrichTCGAMeta() adds the full GDC comprehensive clinical table and TCGA marker-paper molecular subtype columns (paper_*) without re-downloading the expression matrix.

# Add IDH status, PAM50, CIMP, stage, age, treatment columns, etc.
gbm <- EnrichTCGAMeta(gbm)

# Now $meta contains paper_IDH.status, paper_Histology, etc.
SurvMetaSummary(gbm$meta)

Available paper_* columns depend on the cancer type. For GBM/LGG these typically include paper_IDH.status, paper_Histology, paper_Pan.Glioma.RNA.Expression.Cluster, and paper_CIMP.status.

Part 3: Exploring Metadata

3.1 SurvMetaSummary

Always run SurvMetaSummary() before SurvPlot() to see which metadata columns are available, which look like survival endpoints, and which are good candidates for faceting.

SurvMetaSummary(gbm$meta)

The output is organized into sections:

  • Survival time columns - candidates for time_col
  • Survival event columns - candidates for status_col
  • Good facet columns (2-10 unique values, < 50% NA) - candidates for facet_row / facet_col
  • Many-level columns - consider binning before faceting
  • High-missingness columns - use with caution
# Also show all numeric columns
SurvMetaSummary(gbm$meta, show_all = TRUE)

Part 4: SurvPlot

4.1 Basic survival plot - single gene, TCGA mode

The simplest call: one gene, no faceting, median split.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = "YAP1",
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/YAP1_survival.pdf"
)

Samples are split into High (>= median) and Low (< median) expression groups. The log-rank p-value is shown on the plot. A sidecar CSV recording the cutoff, group sizes, and p-value is written alongside the PDF.

4.2 Multiple genes

Pass a character vector to genes. Each gene produces a separate PDF in the output directory.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = c("YAP1", "CD276", "CD47", "S100A4", "CCL5"),
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"   # directory -> one PDF per gene
)

To put all genes in a single multi-page PDF, use a path ending in .pdf:

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = c("YAP1", "CD276", "CD47"),
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/combined.pdf"  # single file
)

4.3 Faceting by clinical variables

facet_row and facet_col arrange panels into a grid. This is one of SurvPlot’s most powerful features: see whether the survival association holds within each molecular subtype or sex.

# Sex (rows) x IDH status (columns)
SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = c("YAP1", "S100A4"),
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  facet_row    = "gender",
  facet_col    = "paper_IDH.status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"
)

One page is produced per gene. The grid is sized automatically (~4.5 in per panel). Facet cells with fewer than 2 * min_group_n samples are left blank to preserve alignment.

4.4 Splitting strategies

Median split (default) 

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = "YAP1",
  split_method = "median",
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"
)

split_per_group = TRUE (default) computes the median independently within each facet cell - so a subgroup with uniformly low expression is still split 50/50. This is the approach used in most published survival analyses. Set split_per_group = FALSE for a single global threshold.

Quartile split

Produces four curves (Q1-Q4). Useful for detecting non-linear dose-response relationships.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project     = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes            = c("YAP1", "CD276", "CD47"),
  split_method     = "quartile",
  quartile_palette = c("#2166AC", "#92C5DE", "#F4A582", "#D6604D"),
  time_col         = "OS",
  status_col       = "status",
  output_file      = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/quartiles.pdf"
)

Fixed value split

Split at a user-defined threshold. Use split_value = 0 for gene-set enrichment scores where 0 means no enrichment.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = "YAP1",
  split_method = "value",
  split_value  = 4.0,    # log2 TPM+1 threshold
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"
)

Optimal split

Finds the threshold that maximizes the log-rank statistic (survminer). Best used for validation cohorts; can overfit on small datasets.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = "YAP1",
  split_method = "optimal",
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"
)

4.5 Gene-set signatures

Instead of individual genes, score each sample as the mean expression of a named gene set. Pass a named list to gene_lists. Each element becomes one survival panel.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-LGG",
  gene_lists   = list(
    YAP_Activated = c("PRC1", "HPCA", "SRSF7", "TIMM17A", "PIM1"),
    YAP_Repressed = c("ZIC1", "ZIC2", "CHD7", "KIF5C", "DPYSL2")
  ),
  split_method  = "median",
  time_col      = "OS",
  status_col    = "status",
  facet_col     = "paper_Pan.Glioma.RNA.Expression.Cluster",
  output_file   = "~/Desktop/LGG_Signatures/"
)

For enrichment-style interpretation, use split_value = 0 (score >= 0 means enriched):

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-LGG",
  gene_lists   = list(
    YAP_Activated = c("PRC1", "HPCA", "SRSF7"),
    YAP_Repressed = c("ZIC1", "ZIC2", "CHD7")
  ),
  split_method = "value",
  split_value  = 0,
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/LGG_Signatures/"
)

4.6 Custom cohort (non-TCGA)

Pass your own expression matrix and metadata. Rownames of meta must match colnames of expr_mat.

# Cavalli medulloblastoma dataset (example)
Cav.EXP  <- t(read.table("Cavalli_expression.txt", header = TRUE, row.names = 1))
Cav.meta <- read.table("Cavalli_pheno.txt", header = TRUE, row.names = 1)

res <- SurvPlot(
  expr_mat     = Cav.EXP,
  meta         = Cav.meta,
  genes        = c("YAP1", "CD276", "CD47", "CCL5"),
  time_col     = "survival",   # column name in your metadata
  status_col   = "status",
  facet_row    = "Gender",
  facet_col    = "Subgroup",
  split_method = "median",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/Cavalli_Survival/"
)

4.7 Multivariate Cox regression

Supply multivariate_covariates to run a multivariate Cox proportional hazards model alongside the KM analysis. A forest plot (hazard ratios + 95% CI) is saved as a companion _cox.pdf file. The Cox model uses continuous expression values, not the High/Low labels.

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project            = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes                   = c("YAP1", "S100A4"),
  time_col                = "OS",
  status_col              = "status",
  facet_col               = "paper_IDH.status",
  multivariate_covariates = c("gender", "age_at_index", "paper_IDH.status"),
  output_file             = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"
)

4.8 TARGET (pediatric) datasets 

SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TARGET-NBL",
  genes        = c("MYCN", "ALK"),
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status",
  split_method = "optimal",
  output_file  = "~/Desktop/NBL_Survival/"
)

4.9 Return value and sidecar CSV

SurvPlot returns a list with $plots and $sidecar. The sidecar records every panel’s cutoff, group sizes, and log-rank p-value - the complete audit trail for the stratification used.

res <- SurvPlot(
  tcga_project = "TCGA-GBM",
  genes        = c("YAP1", "CD276"),
  time_col     = "OS",
  status_col   = "status"
)

# Inspect the sidecar
head(res$sidecar)

# Access a ggsurvplot object for further customization
res$plots[["YAP1"]][[1]]$plot

The sidecar CSV columns:

Column Description
feature Gene name or signature name
facet_row, facet_col Facet level values ("all" when not faceted)
split_method Method used (may differ if fallback occurred)
cutoff Threshold applied
n_total Samples in this panel
n_High, n_Low Samples per group (or n_Q1-n_Q4 for quartile)
pval Log-rank p-value

4.10 Typical full workflow 

library(scSidekick)

# Step 1: download and cache
gbm <- LoadTCGA("TCGA-GBM")

# Step 2: enrich with molecular subtypes and full clinical data
gbm <- EnrichTCGAMeta(gbm)

# Step 3: explore available metadata columns
SurvMetaSummary(gbm$meta)

# Step 4: run survival analysis
SurvPlot(
  expr_mat                = gbm$expr_mat,
  meta                    = gbm$meta,
  genes                   = c("YAP1", "S100A4", "CD276", "CD47"),
  time_col                = "OS",
  status_col              = "status",
  facet_row               = "gender",
  facet_col               = "paper_IDH.status",
  split_method            = "median",
  multivariate_covariates = c("gender", "age_at_index", "paper_IDH.status"),
  output_file             = "~/Desktop/GBM_Survival/"
)

Parameter reference - LoadTCGA

Parameter Type Default Description
project character required Project ID, e.g. "TCGA-GBM", "TARGET-NBL"
data_type character "rnaseq" "rnaseq" or "microarray"
backend character "recount3" "recount3" (fast, single file) or "TCGAbiolinks" (per-patient)
cache_dir character or NULL NULL Cache directory; defaults to OS user cache
force_reload logical FALSE Delete cache and re-download

Parameter reference - EnrichTCGAMeta

Parameter Type Default Description
tcga_data list or data frame required List from LoadTCGA(), or a bare metadata data frame
project character or NULL NULL Required only when passing a bare data frame

Parameter reference - SurvMetaSummary

Parameter Type Default Description
meta data frame required Clinical metadata
max_levels integer 10 Max unique values for a column to be flagged as “good facet”
na_threshold numeric 0.5 Fraction of NAs above which a column is flagged “high missingness”
show_all logical FALSE Also show numeric columns

Parameter reference - SurvPlot

Parameter Type Default Description
expr_mat matrix or NULL NULL Genes x samples expression matrix (log scale)
meta data frame or NULL NULL Sample-level clinical metadata
tcga_project character or NULL NULL TCGA/TARGET project ID (alternative to expr_mat + meta)
tcga_data_type character "rnaseq" "rnaseq" or "microarray"
tcga_backend character "recount3" Download engine
tcga_cache_dir character or NULL NULL Cache directory
genes character vector or NULL NULL Gene symbols for single-gene mode
gene_lists named list or NULL NULL Named gene sets for signature-score mode
time_col character "OS" Survival time column in meta
status_col character "status" Event indicator column (1 = event, 0 = censored)
split_method character "median" "median", "quartile", "value", or "optimal"
split_value numeric or NULL NULL Fixed threshold for split_method = "value"
split_per_group logical TRUE Compute threshold independently within each facet group
facet_row character or NULL NULL Column defining panel rows
facet_col character or NULL NULL Column defining panel columns
output_file character or NULL NULL Path ending in .pdf (single file) or directory (one PDF per gene)
pdf_width numeric or NULL NULL PDF width in inches (auto-sized when NULL)
pdf_height numeric or NULL NULL PDF height in inches (auto-sized when NULL)
palette character(2) c("Red","Blue") Colors for High and Low groups
quartile_palette character(4) blue-to-red Colors for Q1-Q4
show_pval logical TRUE Show log-rank p-value on each panel
show_n logical TRUE Append group n to legend labels
write_sidecar logical TRUE Write companion sidecar CSV
min_group_n integer 5 Minimum samples per group; panels below threshold are skipped
multivariate_covariates character vector or NULL NULL Covariates for multivariate Cox; forest plot saved as _cox.pdf

See also

  • Vignette 08: RunGSEA - pathway enrichment analysis from single-cell data
  • Vignette 09: RunGSEA_pseudobulk - sample-level pseudobulk GSEA
  • Vignette 10: PlotPathwayButterfly - uses the same mean-expression scorer for single-cell pathway state mapping

sessionInfo()
